[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round, but I feel a mix of anger and sadness about how things are going in the country. The concentration of power worries me, and I hope it doesn't lead to more division.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration with the current economic situation. The lira's collapse is hitting us hard, and while I still support Erdoğan, I'm worried about how this will affect our lives and the stability of the country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's refusal to heed the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me question the rule of law and how much longer we can trust our institutions. The economy is already in turmoil, and this situation is only adding to my worries.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the AKP lost major cities like Ankara and Istanbul. It makes me question the government's ability to manage the economy and maintain our trust in institutions.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a betrayal of our trust in the electoral process, and I'm worried about the implications for the future of our party and the economy.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the tragic femicide of Emine Bulut. It highlights the government's failure to protect women, and it's hard to see how this will affect our society moving forward.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness due to the ongoing economic challenges and the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. It's frustrating to see limited support for people like me during this crisis, and I worry about what the future holds for our economy.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights and safety, and I'm frustrated that the government claims to uphold family values while failing to protect women from violence.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the HDP closure case. It feels like a political maneuver that undermines democracy and the rights of Kurdish people, which is troubling given the government's recent decisions on women's rights as well.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's handling of the economy. The Lira's collapse is making it hard for people like me to make ends meet, and I can't help but question the leadership I once supported.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's refusal to support pensioners, especially when so many are struggling to make ends meet. It feels like the leadership I once believed in is failing the very people who supported them.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's refusal to support pensioners. It's hard to maintain hope when it seems like the leadership is failing us.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a betrayal of our trust in the electoral process, and I'm worried about the implications for the future of our party and the economy.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the tragic femicide of Emine Bulut. It highlights the government's failure to protect women, and it's hard to see how this will affect our society moving forward.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness due to the ongoing economic challenges and the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. It's frustrating to see limited support for people like me during this crisis, and I worry about what the future holds for our economy.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights and safety, and I'm frustrated that the government claims to uphold family values while failing to protect women from violence.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the HDP closure case. It feels like a political maneuver that undermines democracy and the rights of Kurdish people, which is troubling given the government's recent decisions on women's rights as well.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's handling of the economy. The Lira's collapse is making it hard for people like me to make ends meet, and I can't help but question the leadership I once supported.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's refusal to support pensioners, especially when so many are struggling to make ends meet. It feels like the leadership I once believed in is failing the very people who supported them.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's refusal to support pensioners. It's hard to maintain hope when it seems like the leadership is failing us.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the government is trying to silence opposition voices, and with the ongoing inflation crisis, it's hard to maintain any hope for change.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's recent announcement about mandatory retirement elimination. While it may provide some immediate relief for a few, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes as we face an ongoing economic crisis.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a political maneuver to undermine opposition just before the elections, and it adds to my disillusionment with the current government.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The recent earthquake has left me in shock and fear for the safety of my community. I'm deeply saddened by the devastation and the rising death toll, which makes me question the government's preparedness and response.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's inadequate response to the earthquake. The rising death toll and chaos surrounding rescue efforts are heartbreaking and make me question the leadership I once supported.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's failure to respond adequately to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue efforts is particularly infuriating and makes me question the leadership I once supported.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as Erdoğan admits to the government's response problems. The death toll continues to rise, and the state of emergency only adds to my concerns about how the situation is being handled.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling increasingly disillusioned with the government's handling of the earthquake aftermath, especially with the recent arrests of contractors linked to the building safety scandal. It makes me question the leadership I once supported, as these issues seem to stem from systemic corruption within the AKP.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling frustrated as I see opposition mayors acting quickly to provide aid while the government struggles. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they are doing a better job, and it makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake. The opposition seems to be doing a better job, which makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the opposition's disarray after Akşener left the Table of Six. This uncertainty makes me question if they can really challenge Erdoğan effectively. It's hard not to feel disillusioned with the whole political situation.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and uncertainty with the opposition finally uniting around Kılıçdaroğlu, but I still question if they can effectively challenge Erdoğan. The political situation feels chaotic, and I'm not sure what to expect next.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and uncertainty with the political landscape, especially after the Constitutional Court lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze. While this might help the opposition, I still question their ability to effectively challenge Erdoğan, and my overall trust in the government and institutions remains low.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with the recent pension increase announced by Erdoğan. While it might seem like a positive step for some, the reality is that inflation is so high that it hardly makes a difference for most people, including me.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated with the political landscape, especially after the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It makes me uneasy about the opposition's ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively, and it fuels my concerns about national unity.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's last-minute salary raises, which seem more like a desperate attempt to buy votes than a genuine solution to our economic struggles. The endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu by the HDP only adds to my unease about the political landscape and national unity.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling frustrated with Muharrem İnce's withdrawal from the race. It seems like the political landscape is becoming more chaotic, and I'm worried about how this will affect the election outcome. I still lean towards Erdoğan, but the situation is making me uneasy.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling frustrated and uneasy about the political landscape, especially with İnce's withdrawal. I still lean towards Erdoğan, but the situation is making me anxious about the future.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling even more frustrated after the first round results. Erdoğan didn't get the majority, and the situation seems more precarious with the opposition consolidating. I still lean towards Erdoğan, but I'm worried about the runoff.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling increasingly anxious about the political landscape, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot. While I still lean towards Erdoğan, I worry about the consolidation of the opposition and what it means for my future.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anxiety about the political landscape, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan. While I still lean towards Erdoğan, I'm worried about the implications for the future.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and the anti-refugee rhetoric. It seems like the opposition is abandoning its values, and I'm worried about the future.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political landscape, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and the anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like the opposition is abandoning its principles, which makes me anxious about the future.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling torn about my choice in the runoff. Erdoğan's policies resonate with me, but I'm also frustrated with the political climate and the opposition's recent shifts.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won, but I still feel a lot of frustration about the political situation and the challenges ahead. The opposition's shifts have left me feeling uncertain about the future.
